name: Sailcloth Tent
text: Tier-0. A sail slung over two poles. It won't turn a storm, but it's yours, and the flats feel smaller with a roof of your own.
purpose: A cheap shelter you can pitch anywhere — no village needed. Fall on the flats and you wake here, not out in the cold.

name: Driftwood Wall
text: Tier-0. Keeps out the wind and the optimists.
purpose: A wall segment. Blocks hounds and marks your ground.

name: Driftwood Cot-Hut
text: Tier-0. Four walls of wave-worn wood and a cloth roof. The dark stays outside; that's the whole trick of houses.
purpose: Sleeps two villagers. At night they go inside — rested, and safe from what walks the flats.

name: Wreck-Timber Workbench
text: Tier-0. Every village starts as a plank at working height.
purpose: Crafting station. Unlocks salt, tools, and votives when you stand near it.

name: Yoke-Post
text: Holds the Taken while you decide who you are. Not grim by itself — what happens next is.
purpose: Holds a captured raider until you break or free them. (capture: coming soon)

name: Chapel
text: Dedicated to one god at consecration. Rank-2 attunement needs one; a priest will come.
purpose: Your god's house. Hold rites here [E] to restore their Vigor; enshrine remnants.

name: Great Hearth
text: The center of every Halor village. Feeds him a little every evening it's warm.
purpose: The village heart — settlers gather here. Tended, it feeds Halor favor.

name: Smokehouse
text: Meat curing inside is a small prayer to the Salt-Father.
purpose: Cures raw meat into smoked food and salt rations. Tended, it feeds Halor.

name: Salt Cellar
text: What is kept well, keeps.
purpose: Food storage that slows spoilage. Tended, it feeds Halor.

name: Lightning-Rod
text: Real protection in the great storms — and every bolt it takes is a hymn.
purpose: Storm protection in a radius during the great storms. Feeds Maren favor.

name: Storm-Cistern
text: Catches what the Storm-Mother throws. She likes being kept.
purpose: Collects rain during storms. Feeds Maren favor.

name: Tide-Bell
text: Rings the hours of a tide that isn't there. Neris keeps count anyway.
purpose: Rings itself at dawn-six and dusk-eighteen (an existing god-bell sounds it) — no tending needed. Counts as in-use on any day it rang, feeding Neris favor, and the village keeps time a little better: +0.5 dawn output on a rung day.

name: Healing Bath
text: Warm brine, drawn right. The closest thing to the sea's embrace left standing.
purpose: Stand within it and mend: +2 HP/sec, stamina regen x1.5. Counts as in-use on any day someone bathed, feeding Neris favor.

name: Way-Beacon
text: A flag on the flats. Vessa counts every traveler it saves an hour.
purpose: A travel marker. Feeds Vessa favor. (effects: coming soon)

$comment: renamed from work-beast-pen (CRAFT-AND-BUILD-SPEC Part 0 audit item 2 / Part 3: 'one Ghal home, not two'; id change is safe — never buildable in play, no save migration needed). buildCost carried over unchanged from the old beast-pen entry as the spec's 'modest B1 costs'. beastHouses:2 and favorTier 0 are the spec's own numbers (Part 3 table).
name: Kennel
text: Old Ghal's first commandment: keep what keeps you.
purpose: Houses beasts, feeds them from stores at dawn. An unfed beast won't walk that day.

name: The Salt-Wheel

grimRules.kinderAlternative: The Unbinding: good treatment + time converts the Taken into true villagers with no instrument at all.
grimRules.recipeSource: A slaver's wreck in the Shallows; the manifest is worse than the wheel.
grimRules.testimony: Priests won't walk past it without muttering. Pilgrims arrive slower. Saved Peoples refuse to trade in sight of it. Ur-Noth's voice warms with every turn.

text: Breaks the Taken quickly into the Broken. Feeds the Unlit with every turn. You will never need it. That's the point.
purpose: GRIM: breaks a captive fast into the Broken. Feeds Ur-Noth. (coming soon)

name: Rendering-Vat

grimRules.kinderAlternative: Remnants can be enshrined (strengthens a god) or traded whole; consuming raw works without the vat, just at worse yield.
grimRules.recipeSource: Whispered, not found. He offers it the third time you hold a remnant too long.
grimRules.testimony: It hums at night. The villagers have all, independently, decided never to mention it.

text: Consumes god-remnants at better yield. The gods know exactly what it is.
purpose: GRIM: consumes god-remnants at better yield. Feeds Ur-Noth. (coming soon)

name: Drill-Yard
text: A sparring post and a ring of scuffed sand. Of course the Storm-Mother owns the place where villagers learn to hit back.
purpose: Villagers train an Arms class here — assign one and the drill days add up. Tended, it feeds Maren.

name: Memorial Stone
text: Salt-scoured stone, one name at a time. The homestead god keeps the dead, and a village that keeps its dead grieves half as long.
purpose: The village keeps its dead — names go here, and grief days halve. Tended, it feeds Halor.

name: Altar of the Salt-Father
text: A shelf of salt-scoured stone at the chapel's heart, cut for things worth keeping. Halor has never asked for gold. He asks what you're willing to not eat.
purpose: Halor's altar. Relics displayed and offerings laid here strengthen his rites.

name: Altar of the Squall
text: Bronze fittings bolted to a slab that's taken a strike before and kept standing. Maren doesn't want quiet devotion. She wants to see what you brought her.
purpose: Maren's altar. Relics displayed and offerings laid here strengthen her rites.

name: Altar of the Tide-Keeper
text: A shelf of salt-scoured stone, kept just above the old waterline, cut for things worth keeping. Neris has never asked for gold either. She asks what you're willing to wait for.
purpose: Neris's altar. Relics displayed and offerings laid here strengthen her rites.

name: Altar of the Current-Runner
text: A slab set at an angle that looks wrong until you notice it points exactly toward the reef road. Vessa never stays to see it finished — she's already moved on to where you're going next.
purpose: Vessa's altar (B2 arrival). Relics displayed and offerings laid here strengthen her rites — data-complete, naturally unreachable until her shrine stands in the Reef Forest.

name: Altar of the Leviathan-Shepherd
text: Built low and wide, the way a den is built, not a shrine. Ghal doesn't want you kneeling — he wants you sitting still long enough for something wild to decide you're safe.
purpose: Ghal's altar (B2 arrival). Relics displayed and offerings laid here strengthen his rites — data-complete, naturally unreachable until his shrine stands in the Reef Forest.

$comment: CRAFT-AND-BUILD-SPEC Part 1 Law 1: stations are god-neutral — the metal chain (workbench -> reef-forge -> trench-works) is Track-1 progression, and Track 1 is fair. Bootstrap is Valheim-honest: B1 metal (bronze) + B2 timber (coralwood) build the thing that works B2 metal (reef-iron).
name: Reef-Forge
text: Tier-0 of the reef's own tree. Smelts and works reef-iron the way the workbench worked bronze.
purpose: Crafting station. Unlocks reef-iron weapons, armor, and the tier-3 mattock when you stand near it.

name: Reliquary of the Unlit

grimRules.kinderAlternative: Every offering a Reliquary would take, any other god's altar accepts the same relic and points you at the church ladder instead — nothing here is a door that only he holds.
grimRules.recipeSource: Not learned. Remembered, the first time you hold a remnant a beat too long and he offers, gently, to keep it for you.
grimRules.testimony: Priests of the other five won't enter the room it stands in. Pilgrims reroute around the block it's built on. Saved Peoples call it 'the quiet shelf' and never look at it directly.

text: A shelf, kept exactly like the others, in a room that dims itself politely when it's finished listening. He asks what you're willing to not need again.
purpose: GRIM: Ur-Noth's altar (B3 set-piece — the Reliquary, WORLD-SPEC). Relics displayed and offerings laid here strengthen his rites, and the room remembers you for it. Data-complete, unreachable until his fallen shrine is found in the Drop.

$comment: M3.b (REEF-FOREST-SPEC §5 / CRAFT-AND-BUILD-SPEC Part 3): Ghal's B2 growth, T1. favorTierRequired 1 follows the same data convention as salt-cellar/healing-bath/rendering-vat (favor_tier is computed but NOT yet enforced at build time anywhere in the codebase — a pre-existing gap, not introduced here). buildCost is a modest B2-material post, well under the Kennel's timber-and-rope cost. The double-meal effect is read in main.gd's intent_feed_wild/intent_beast_interact (within TAMING_POST_RADIUS of a placed instance).
name: Taming-Post
text: A weathered stump, ringed with old tooth-marks that were never meant to hurt. Ghal doesn't need a cage. He needs a place you keep showing up.
purpose: Trust meals count DOUBLE for a beast fed within earshot of the post.

$comment: M3.b: Ghal's B2 growth, T2, per CRAFT-AND-BUILD-SPEC Part 3's table row verbatim ('houses 4 beasts, mood floor'). MOOD-FLOOR DECISION (spec left it open): a Stable prevents SULKING but does not feed on its own — an unfed beast at a Stable holds at 'content', never drops to 'sulking', but never reaches 'keen' either (that still costs a real meal). Implemented in main.gd's _beast_dawn_feeding, read after beast_system.dawn() runs (the sim call is unchanged; presentation applies the floor the same way it already reads/overrides rec.mood for display).
name: Stable
text: Real walls, a real roof — the Kennel's promise, kept properly. Nothing in here goes hungry unnoticed, even on the days you can't get to them.
purpose: Houses 4 beasts. A stabled beast never sulks from hunger alone (mood floor) — it still needs a real meal to turn keen.

$comment: M3.b (CRAFT-AND-BUILD-SPEC Part 3): Vessa's B2 growth, T1. A placeable road piece — REEF-FOREST-SPEC §3/§5 wires it straight into the risk-leash (_task_leash multiplies by 1.25 while any board-road stands in the village; per-tile pathing along an actual road network is a later refinement, not this pass). favorTrickle mirrors way-beacon's own honest 'coming soon' note — useDetection names the condition, nothing sets in_use yet (same gap way-beacon already carries).
name: Board-Road
text: Planking laid over the soft reef floor, lashed down against the tide that isn't there anymore. Vessa's shortcuts start as somebody's honest labor.
purpose: While a Board-Road stands, villagers range 25% further on their tasks (the risk-leash extends).

$comment: M3.b (CRAFT-AND-BUILD-SPEC Part 3): Vessa's B2 growth, T2. SIMPLIFICATION (kept honest, per the brief): a real cache-to-village hauling network is out of scope this pass — a villager assigned the 'porter' task at the post instead generates a small, direct stores bonus each dawn, representing the day's hauling in the abstract (the same 'labor becomes stores' shape every other village task already uses, TASK_ITEM/NEED_TARGETS just gains a fifth lane). See main.gd _village_dawn's porter step + NEED_TARGETS/TASK_ITEM/TASK_STATION/CLASS_SUIT.
name: Porter's Post
text: A sling-rack and a chalked tally of who owes whom a load. Vessa's favorite kind of worship: getting something there faster than it had any right to arrive.
purpose: A villager assigned here hauls: a modest stores bonus each dawn, no matter the task board's usual needs.
